mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates indicate an accommodative monetary policy stance, reducing the cost of capital and encouraging investment, which translates to higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat (median return < 0.001) during periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over the medium term.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust housing starts indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand, suggesting a healthy economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns in the short term.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3-month average growth > 2%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative or flat (median return < 0.001) when both RSAFS and HOUST show strong growth for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, an accelerating industrial production index indicates a rebound in manufacturing and supply-side activity, which boosts corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a medium horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth above 3% during post-recession periods does not correlate with positive S&P 500 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant contraction in real GDP signals a severe economic downturn, leading to widespread corporate profit declines and increased risk aversion, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer investment horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of instances where GDPC1 contracts by more than 1.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, supporting consumer confidence and spending, which in turn drives corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: If the correlation between PAYEMS growth and S&P 500 20-day returns is consistently negative or zero (Rank IC < 0.01) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating inflation combined with rising federal funds rates indicates a hawkish monetary policy response to overheating, which increases discount rates and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns in the short term.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 4% AND FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25% in a month
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where both CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 4% and FEDFUNDS rises by 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high consumer confidence, strong retail sales growth indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a medium horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth above 0.3% during periods of high consumer confidence does not lead to positive S&P 500 20-day returns (median return < 0.001).
